title: Music You Associate With Eva and Eva Characters
display_title: Music You Associate With Eva and Eva Characters
wikitext:
[]: Music You Associate With Eva and Eva Characters             Quote Post by Barinax » Sat Jul 26, 2008 11:54 pm And does the music in the series seem to be different from what he listens to in Rebuild?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 27, 2008 2:17 am Video game image albums, Masami Okui, and classical music. [/smart-ass response] Regarding Rebuild, the music cue sheet in the Big Black 1.0 Book lists the SDAT songs as "Aoi Legend", "Aoi Legend", and... er... "Aoi Legend"... This is one of the numbers Shinji listens to in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jul 27, 2008 1:31 pm I thought he still listened to "You Are the Only One" during the spacing out in the monorail scene of Rebuild?

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Jul 27, 2008 1:33 pm Raining Blood - Slayer

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jul 27, 2008 1:35 pm Iron Rooster wrote: Raining Blood - Slayer

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 27, 2008 5:49 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I thought he still listened to "You Are the Only One" during the spacing out in the monorail scene of Rebuild?

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Jul 27, 2008 7:20 pm Shinji listens to talk radio.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Jul 27, 2008 8:52 pm Shinji would probably have "Raining Blood" play while Unit 01 rips Leliel apart.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sun Jul 27, 2008 9:39 pm "Hero of the Day" by Metallica

[]:             Quote Post by Zkh » Sun Jul 27, 2008 9:45 pm Incense and Peppermints by Strawberry Alarm Clock Power of Love by Celine Dion When A Man Loves a Woman - Milly Vanilli & the backyard gang Runescape Theme song by R. Kelly (personal fave)

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Jul 27, 2008 9:58 pm When Shinji catches Sahaquiel, I can sooooo see him listening to Planet Hell by Nightwish.

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Sun Jul 27, 2008 11:17 pm The Who's "Behind Blue Eyes," perhaps?

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Jul 27, 2008 11:20 pm waaaaat? A fourteen year old kid living in a city listening to music that's 40 years older than he and in another language? I really doubt that. I really, really doubt that. So I throw in The Wall. That's only 30 years younger than he.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Mon Jul 28, 2008 12:13 am http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=21zzMkUePow end of DISCUSSION lol

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Jul 28, 2008 12:27 am PUH-lease, I mean, come on. Can't we at least be realistic? I think my choice was a good one earlier. Try listening to the lyrics of it.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Jul 28, 2008 10:31 am From his mentality, any music conveying the end, the "unto nothing" kind of music (elegiac music to be more precise). Don't call me an old buffoon, but in the classic genre, it would most likely be Ricahrd Strauss, Wagner, or Mahler. But as he was playing Bach on his cello, maybe he might like some of good Baroque composers, which I cannot really concieve specific composers, but more like specific piece, which I am convinced would be quite a few.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jul 28, 2008 10:48 am Good point, Imp. Shinji does strike me as the type to listen to Classical music (Listening to the 9th after meeting Kaworu, hehe).

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Jul 28, 2008 10:59 am It's just that the 9th Symphony, just doesn't work with his mentality. The Gemran choral text completely contradicts his uh, mood, never mind the music itself, unless all that rejoice and admittance into 'Elysium' (quoting from Schiller) is what he wants. It first sounds dooming, (first movement), but it is the journey of rejoice, in gaining happiness through through working. No despair, mind you. This is not explicitly what he wants, and he does seem to prefer the gradual descent into release from suffering through 'expiring', not release from suffering through entering paradise. Therefore, Shinji, in my view, would probably prefer the self destructing nature of the German legends, which may paint a picture, which i do admit, seems to resonate with our own deep mentality. It is from my view that Japanese people, through their deepe seated Buddhist culture, seeks to disappear, to expire to be relieved from their suffering through death. Rather like the very Gothic culture of the Germans.

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Mon Jul 28, 2008 12:51 pm I could see Shinji digging synthpop a la Pet Shop Boys, Flock of Seagulls or Cut Copy, maybe even Das Ich.

[]:             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Wed Aug 06, 2008 5:19 pm For some reason I associate the albums Violator by Depeche Mode and The Downward Spiral by Nine Inch Nails with him.
